question: Which matches ended in a draw, and what were The participating teams and their scores?
sql_answer: SELECT m.match_api_id, t1.team_long_name AS home_team, t2.team_long_name AS away_team, m.home_team_goal, m.away_team_goal FROM `match` m JOIN team t1 ON m.home_team_api_id = t1.team_api_id JOIN team t2 ON m.away_team_api_id = t2.team_api_id WHERE m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal ORDER BY m.date DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Calculate the number of superheroes that belong to Each publisher.
sql_answer: SELECT p.publisher_name, COUNT(s.id) AS hero_count FROM publisher p JOIN superhero s ON p.id = s.publisher_id GROUP BY p.publisher_name ORDER BY hero_count DESC;

question: Identify the constructors whose drivers accumulated the Highest number of race points.
sql_answer: SELECT c.name, SUM(r.points) AS total_points FROM constructors c JOIN results r ON c.constructorId = r.constructorId GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY total_points DESC LIMIT 10;
notes: Using COUNT instead of SUM; incorrect table alias

question: Compute the total revenue generated by each gas station based on fuel sales.
sql_answer: SELECT d.forename, d.surname, SUM(r.points) AS total_points FROM drivers d JOIN results r ON d.driverId = r.driverId GROUP BY d.driverId ORDER BY total_points DESC LIMIT 10;

question: List the drivers who scored points in races and Display their total points.
sql_answer: SELECT d.forename,d.surname,SUM(r.points) AS total_pointsFROM drivers dJOIN results r ON d.driverId = r.driverIdGROUP BY d.driverIdORDER BY total_points DESCLIMIT 10;

question: Which drivers have an average race score higher Than the overall average race score across all drivers?
sql_answer: SELECT d.driverRef, AVG(r.points) AS avg_points FROM drivers d JOIN results r ON d.driverId = r.driverId GROUP BY d.driverId HAVING AVG(r.points) > (SELECT AVG(points) FROM results);

question: Display the football teams with the highest average player rating.
sql_answer: SELECT t.team_long_name, AVG(pa.overall_rating) AS avg_rating FROM team t JOIN `match` m ON t.team_api_id = m.home_team_api_id JOIN player p ON m.home_player_1 = p.player_api_id JOIN player_attributes pa ON p.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id GROUP BY t.team_long_name ORDER BY avg_rating DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Which users have received more votes on their posts than the average number of votes received by users?
sql_answer: SELECT u.Id, COUNT(v.Id) AS votes_received FROM users u JOIN posts p ON u.Id = p.OwnerUserId JOIN votes v ON p.Id = v.PostId GROUP BY u.Id HAVING COUNT(v.Id) > (SELECT AVG(vote_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) vote_count FROM votes GROUP BY UserId) x);

question: Which drivers have won more races than the average number of wins among all drivers, and for which constructors did those wins occur?
sql_answer: SELECT d.driverRef, c.name AS constructor, COUNT(*) AS wins FROM drivers d JOIN results r ON d.driverId = r.driverId JOIN constructors c ON r.constructorId = c.constructorId WHERE r.position = 1 GROUP BY d.driverId, c.constructorId HAVING COUNT(*) > (SELECT AVG(win_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS win_count FROM results WHERE position = 1 GROUP BY driverId) x);

question: Which schools are "underdogs"—meaning they belong to a district where the average SAT score is below 500, but the school itself managed to score in the top 5% of the entire state?
sql_answer: SELECT s.School, s.District, sa.AvgScrMath, (SELECT AVG(sa2.AvgScrMath) FROM satscores sa2 JOIN schools s2 ON sa2.cds = s2.CDSCode WHERE s2.District = s.District) AS district_avg FROM schools s JOIN satscores sa ON s.CDSCode = sa.cds WHERE sa.AvgScrMath > (SELECT AVG(AvgScrMath) + (1.645 * STDDEV(AvgScrMath)) FROM satscores) AND (SELECT AVG(sa3.AvgScrMath) FROM satscores sa3 JOIN schools s3 ON sa3.cds = s3.CDSCode WHERE s3.District = s.District) < 500 ORDER BY sa.AvgScrMath DESC;
notes: Missing ORDER BY; aggregate used with non-aggregated column

question: Which card sets have the most cards with isFoil=1 and also have highest average convertedManaCost?
sql_answer: SELECT se.name AS set_name, COUNT(c.id) AS foil_count, AVG(c.convertedManaCost) AS avg_cmc FROM cards c JOIN sets se ON c.setCode = se.code JOIN legalities l ON c.uuid = l.uuid WHERE c.hasFoil = 1 GROUP BY se.name ORDER BY foil_count DESC, avg_cmc DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Which gas stations generated the highest total revenue from transactions?
sql_answer: SELECT g.GasStationID, COUNT(t.TransactionID) AS total_transactions, SUM(t.Amount * t.Price) AS revenue FROM transactions_1k t JOIN gasstations g ON t.GasStationID = g.GasStationID JOIN products p ON t.ProductID = p.ProductID GROUP BY g.GasStationID ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Which drivers finished races in the top 3 positions and what constructors did they drive for?
sql_answer: SELECT d.forename, d.surname, c.name AS constructor, r.name AS race, res.position FROM drivers d JOIN results res ON d.driverId = res.driverId JOIN constructors c ON res.constructorId = c.constructorId JOIN races r ON res.raceId = r.raceId WHERE res.position <= 3 ORDER BY r.year DESC;

question: Which superheroes have more powers than the average number of powers per superhero and what publisher are they associated with?
sql_answer: SELECT s.superhero_name, p.publisher_name, COUNT(hp.power_id) AS total_powers FROM superhero s JOIN hero_power hp ON s.id = hp.hero_id JOIN publisher p ON s.publisher_id = p.id GROUP BY s.id, p.id HAVING COUNT(hp.power_id) > (SELECT AVG(power_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) power_count FROM hero_power GROUP BY hero_id) x);

question: Find the clients who performed the highest number of transactions and calculate the total amount they transferred.
sql_answer: SELECT c.client_id, COUNT(t.trans_id) AS total_transactions, SUM(t.amount) AS total_amount FROM client c JOIN disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id JOIN account a ON d.account_id = a.account_id JOIN trans t ON a.account_id = t.account_id GROUP BY c.client_id ORDER BY total_transactions DESC LIMIT 10;
notes: Incorrect ORDER BY column; missing JOIN

question: Which clients have accounts with more than 20 transactions and what is their total transaction amount?
sql_answer: SELECT c.client_id, COUNT(t.trans_id) AS transaction_count, SUM(t.amount) AS total_amount FROM client c JOIN disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id JOIN account a ON d.account_id = a.account_id JOIN trans t ON a.account_id = t.account_id GROUP BY c.client_id HAVING COUNT(t.trans_id) > 20;

question: Which gas stations have customers with the highest cumulative transactions and repeated visits?
sql_answer: SELECT g.GasStationID, g.ChainID, COUNT(DISTINCT t.CustomerID) AS unique_customers, SUM(t.Amount) AS total_spent, COUNT(t.TransactionID) AS total_visits FROM transactions_1k t JOIN gasstations g ON t.GasStationID = g.GasStationID JOIN customers c ON t.CustomerID = c.CustomerID JOIN yearmonth y ON y.CustomerID = c.CustomerID GROUP BY g.GasStationID, g.ChainID ORDER BY total_spent DESC, total_visits DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Which football teams played matches where the total Goals scored were higher than the league average?
sql_answer: WITH avg_goals AS (SELECT AVG(home_team_goal + away_team_goal) AS avg_goal FROM `match`) SELECT t.team_long_name, COUNT(m.match_api_id) AS high_scoring_matches FROM `match` m JOIN team t ON m.home_team_api_id = t.team_api_id JOIN league l ON m.league_id = l.id JOIN country c ON l.country_id = c.id JOIN avg_goals ag WHERE (m.home_team_goal + m.away_team_goal) > ag.avg_goal GROUP BY t.team_long_name ORDER BY high_scoring_matches DESC;

question: Which publishers have a "Power Monopoly"? Find publishers who own more than 40% of all superheroes that possess a specific "God-tier" power (powers held by fewer than 10 heroes in total).
sql_answer: SELECT p.publisher_name, sp2.power_name, COUNT(s.id) AS hero_count, (COUNT(s.id) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hero_power hp2 WHERE hp2.power_id = hp.power_id)) AS market_share FROM publisher p JOIN superhero s ON p.id = s.publisher_id JOIN hero_power hp ON s.id = hp.hero_id JOIN superpower sp2 ON hp.power_id = sp2.id WHERE hp.power_id IN (SELECT power_id FROM hero_power GROUP BY power_id HAVING COUNT(hero_id) < 10) GROUP BY p.id, p.publisher_name, sp2.power_name, hp.power_id HAVING market_share > 40 ORDER BY market_share DESC;

question: Which publishers own "rare" superheroes—defined as characters who possess a superpower that fewer than 5% of all superheroes in the database have?
sql_answer: SELECT p.publisher_name, s.superhero_name, sp2.power_name FROM superhero s JOIN publisher p ON s.publisher_id = p.id JOIN hero_power hp ON s.id = hp.hero_id JOIN superpower sp2 ON hp.power_id = sp2.id WHERE hp.power_id IN (SELECT power_id FROM hero_power GROUP BY power_id HAVING COUNT(hero_id) < (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM superhero) * 0.05) ORDER BY p.publisher_name ASC;

question: Determine the districts with the largest transactionVolume and number of clients.
sql_answer: SELECT d.district_id, COUNT(DISTINCT c.client_id) AS clients, SUM(t.amount) AS transaction_volume FROM district d JOIN client c ON d.district_id = c.district_id JOIN disp di ON c.client_id = di.client_id JOIN account a ON di.account_id = a.account_id JOIN trans t ON a.account_id = t.account_id GROUP BY d.district_id ORDER BY transaction_volume DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Which publishers have the most diverse superheroes in terms of their moral alignment, gender representation, and the total variety of unique superpowers they possess? Rank them by the count of unique powers.
sql_answer: SELECT p.publisher_name, COUNT(DISTINCT s.alignment_id) AS distinct_alignments, COUNT(DISTINCT s.gender_id) AS distinct_genders, COUNT(DISTINCT hp.power_id) AS unique_powers_count, COUNT(s.id) AS total_superheroes FROM publisher p JOIN superhero s ON p.id = s.publisher_id JOIN hero_power hp ON s.id = hp.hero_id GROUP BY p.id, p.publisher_name ORDER BY unique_powers_count DESC, distinct_alignments DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Which superheroes have more powers than the average number of powers per superhero and what publisher are they associated with?
sql_answer: SELECT s.superhero_name, p.publisher_name, COUNT(hp.power_id) AS total_powers FROM superhero s JOIN hero_power hp ON s.id = hp.hero_id JOIN publisher p ON s.publisher_id = p.id GROUP BY s.id, p.id HAVING COUNT(hp.power_id) > (SELECT AVG(power_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) power_count FROM hero_power GROUP BY hero_id) x);
notes: Column does not exist; typo in table name

question: Which districts have a total sum of transactions greater than the average total transaction amount per account across the entire database, and what are the number of distinct clients, total transaction amount, and average loan amount for those districts?
schema_name: Customed (district, client, disp, account, trans, loan)
sql_answer: SELECT d.district_id, COUNT(DISTINCT c.client_id) AS clients, SUM(t.amount) AS total_transactions, AVG(l.amount) AS avg_loan FROM district d JOIN client c ON d.district_id = c.district_id JOIN disp di ON c.client_id = di.client_id JOIN account a ON di.account_id = a.account_id JOIN trans t ON a.account_id = t.account_id LEFT JOIN loan l ON a.account_id = l.account_id GROUP BY d.district_id HAVING total_transactions > (SELECT AVG(total_sum) FROM (SELECT SUM(amount) total_sum FROM trans GROUP BY account_id) t);

question: Which districts have clients with the highest average Account balance and also have active loans?
schema_name: Customed(district, client, disp, account, trans, loan)
sql_answer: WITH account_balance AS (SELECT account_id, MAX(balance) AS latest_balance FROM trans GROUP BY account_id) SELECT d.district_id, AVG(ab.latest_balance) AS avg_balance, COUNT(DISTINCT l.loan_id) AS loan_count FROM district d JOIN client c ON d.district_id = c.district_id JOIN disp di ON c.client_id = di.client_id JOIN account a ON di.account_id = a.account_id JOIN account_balance ab ON a.account_id = ab.account_id LEFT JOIN loan l ON a.account_id = l.account_id GROUP BY d.district_id ORDER BY avg_balance DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Which football teams have the most dominant performance considering wins, goals, player ratings, and team Tactical attributes?
schema_name: Custom(match, team, player, player_attributes, Team_attributes, league, country)
sql_answer: SELECT t.team_long_name, COUNT(m.match_api_id) AS matches, SUM(CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal > m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS wins, AVG(pa.overall_rating) AS avg_player_rating, AVG(ta.buildUpPlaySpeed) AS avg_speed, AVG(ta.chanceCreationShooting) AS avg_attack FROM `match` m JOIN team t ON m.home_team_api_id = t.team_api_id JOIN league l ON m.league_id = l.id JOIN country c ON l.country_id = c.id JOIN team_attributes ta ON t.team_api_id = ta.team_api_id JOIN player p ON m.home_player_1 = p.player_api_id JOIN player_attributes pa ON p.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id GROUP BY t.team_long_name ORDER BY wins DESC, avg_player_rating DESC LIMIT 10;
notes: Using INNER JOIN instead of  JOIN;

question: Find the top 5 drivers who recorded the fastest laps inraces but whose constructors scored the least total points.
schema_name: Customed(drivers, driverstandings, results, constructorstandings, constructors, races,laptimes)
sql_answer: SELECT d.forename, d.surname, r.name AS race_name, l.milliseconds AS fastest_lap_time, cs.points AS constructor_points, c.name AS constructor_name FROM drivers d JOIN results res ON d.driverId = res.driverId JOIN races r ON res.raceId = r.raceId JOIN laptimes l ON d.driverId = l.driverId AND r.raceId = l.raceId JOIN constructorstandings cs ON res.constructorId = cs.constructorId AND r.raceId = cs.raceId JOIN constructors c ON cs.constructorId = c.constructorId WHERE l.position = 1 ORDER BY cs.points ASC, l.milliseconds ASC LIMIT 5;

question: Which districts generate the highest financial activity considering number of clients, accounts, transactions, Loans, cards, and average balances?
schema_name: Custom (district, client, disp, account, trans, loan, card)
sql_answer: WITH balance_table AS (SELECT account_id, MAX(balance) AS last_balance FROM trans GROUP BY account_id) SELECT d.district_id, COUNT(DISTINCT c.client_id) AS total_clients, COUNT(DISTINCT a.account_id) AS accounts, COUNT(DISTINCT ca.card_id) AS cards, COUNT(DISTINCT l.loan_id) AS loans, SUM(t.amount) AS transaction_volume, AVG(bt.last_balance) AS avg_balance FROM district d JOIN client c ON d.district_id = c.district_id JOIN disp di ON c.client_id = di.client_id JOIN account a ON di.account_id = a.account_id JOIN trans t ON a.account_id = t.account_id LEFT JOIN loan l ON a.account_id = l.account_id LEFT JOIN card ca ON di.disp_id = ca.disp_id LEFT JOIN balance_table bt ON a.account_id = bt.account_id GROUP BY d.district_id ORDER BY transaction_volume DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Which districts have the highest financial activity including number of clients, total transactions, loans, and average transaction value?
schema_name: Customed(district, client, disp, account, trans, loan, card)
sql_answer: SELECT d.district_id, COUNT(DISTINCT c.client_id) AS clients, COUNT(DISTINCT a.account_id) AS accounts, COUNT(t.trans_id) AS transactions, SUM(t.amount) AS total_volume, AVG(t.amount) AS avg_transaction, COUNT(DISTINCT l.loan_id) AS loans, COUNT(DISTINCT ca.card_id) AS cards FROM district d JOIN client c ON d.district_id = c.district_id JOIN disp di ON c.client_id = di.client_id JOIN account a ON di.account_id = a.account_id JOIN trans t ON a.account_id = t.account_id LEFT JOIN loan l ON a.account_id = l.account_id LEFT JOIN card ca ON di.disp_id = ca.disp_id GROUP BY d.district_id ORDER BY total_volume DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Which Formula 1 drivers demonstrate the best race performance considering race results, fastest laps, pit stops, constructor standings, and championship points?
schema_name: Custom (drivers, results, races, constructors, Driverstandings, constructorstandings, Laptimes, pitstops)
sql_answer: WITH driver_stats AS (SELECT d.driverId, AVG(res.position) AS avg_finish, MIN(l.milliseconds) AS best_lap, AVG(ps.milliseconds) AS avg_pitstop, SUM(res.points) AS race_points FROM drivers d JOIN results res ON d.driverId = res.driverId JOIN races r ON res.raceId = r.raceId JOIN laptimes l ON d.driverId = l.driverId AND r.raceId = l.raceId JOIN pitstops ps ON d.driverId = ps.driverId AND r.raceId = ps.raceId GROUP BY d.driverId) SELECT d.forename, d.surname, ds.avg_finish, ds.best_lap, ds.avg_pitstop, ds.race_points, SUM(cs.points) AS constructor_points FROM driver_stats ds JOIN drivers d ON ds.driverId = d.driverId JOIN results res ON d.driverId = res.driverId JOIN constructors c ON res.constructorId = c.constructorId JOIN constructorstandings cs ON c.constructorId = cs.constructorId GROUP BY d.driverId ORDER BY ds.race_points DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Which publishers produce superheroes with the most diverse powers, attributes, alignments, And physical characteristics?
schema_name: Custom (superhero, hero_power, superpower, superhero_attribute, attribute, publisher, alignment, gender, colour)
sql_answer: SELECT p.publisher_name, COUNT(DISTINCT s.id) AS heroes, COUNT(DISTINCT hp.power_id) AS powers, COUNT(DISTINCT sa.attribute_id) AS attributes, COUNT(DISTINCT s.alignment_id) AS alignments, COUNT(DISTINCT s.gender_id) AS genders FROM superhero s JOIN hero_power hp ON s.id = hp.hero_id JOIN superpower sp ON hp.power_id = sp.id JOIN hero_attribute sa ON s.id = sa.hero_id JOIN attribute a ON sa.attribute_id = a.id JOIN publisher p ON s.publisher_id = p.id JOIN alignment al ON s.alignment_id = al.id JOIN gender g ON s.gender_id = g.id LEFT JOIN colour ec ON s.eye_colour_id = ec.id LEFT JOIN colour hc ON s.hair_colour_id = hc.id LEFT JOIN colour sc ON s.skin_colour_id = sc.id GROUP BY p.publisher_name ORDER BY powers DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Which schools demonstrate the strongest academic outcomes considering SAT scores, enrollment, And free meal eligibility?
sql_answer: WITH academic_score AS (SELECT cds, AVG(AvgScrMath + AvgScrRead + AvgScrWrite) AS total_sat FROM satscores GROUP BY cds) SELECT s.School, s.District, a.total_sat, AVG(f.`Percent (%) Eligible FRPM (K-12)`) AS poverty_rate, AVG(f.`Enrollment (K-12)`) AS enrollment FROM schools s JOIN frpm f ON s.CDSCode = f.CDSCode JOIN academic_score a ON s.CDSCode = a.cds GROUP BY s.School, s.District, a.total_sat ORDER BY total_sat DESC LIMIT 10;

question: Find the top 5 drivers who consistently perform above average by comparing their average finish position with the global average, while also considering their fastest lap and total points.
sql_answer: WITH driver_performance AS (SELECT d.driverId, AVG(res.position) AS avg_finish, MIN(l.milliseconds) AS fastest_lap, SUM(res.points) AS total_points FROM drivers d JOIN results res ON d.driverId = res.driverId JOIN races r ON res.raceId = r.raceId JOIN laptimes l ON d.driverId = l.driverId AND r.raceId = l.raceId JOIN constructors c ON res.constructorId = c.constructorId GROUP BY d.driverId), global_avg AS (SELECT AVG(position) AS avg_position FROM results) SELECT d.forename, d.surname, dp.avg_finish, dp.fastest_lap, dp.total_points FROM driver_performance dp JOIN drivers d ON dp.driverId = d.driverId JOIN global_avg g WHERE dp.avg_finish < g.avg_position ORDER BY dp.total_points DESC, dp.fastest_lap ASC LIMIT 5;
